demographics: Julia Parker is a 19-year-old female college student.
history: Chronic irregular menses/oligomenorrhea since menarche, now approximately 5 months of amenorrhea.
Cycles occur about 3-4 times yearly, vary from 21-90 days, and can last 3-4 weeks with heavy bleeding and clots.
Acne and progressive facial hair growth/hirsutism.
No major diet, exercise, or weight change; no abdominal pain, nausea, or abnormal vaginal discharge.
Family history includes obesity, type 2 diabetes, infertility, hypothyroidism, hypercholesterolemia, and cardiac disease.
physical_exam: Moderate acne on face, chest, and back; mild upper-lip and chin hirsutism (Ferriman-Gallwey 8).
Acanthosis nigricans over neck, axilla, and groin.
Oily skin, hair thinning, stretch marks; Tanner stage 5; normal pelvic examination.
labs: Urine and quantitative pregnancy testing are negative.
TSH 6 mU/L with normal free T4 1.3.
LH 18 and FSH 6 (LH greater than FSH); estradiol 15 (low).
Total testosterone 70 and free testosterone 7.8 are elevated; SHBG 4 is low.
Prolactin 11 and 17-hydroxyprogesterone 110 are normal; DHEAS is within reference range.
Triglycerides 169 and LDL 129 are elevated; HDL 38 is low; HbA1c 5.6.
imaging: Pelvic ultrasound shows enlarged ovaries with 12 or more 2-9 mm peripheral follicles and increased central stroma (polycystic ovarian morphology).
diagnosis_and_management: Likely polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS): anovulation/oligomenorrhea, hyperandrogenism, and polycystic ovarian morphology.
Management includes a combined oral contraceptive, metformin, weight management, regular exercise, and a healthy diet.
Relevant risks include dyslipidemia, insulin resistance/metabolic disease, and unopposed-estrogen endometrial risk.

female_hormonal_cycle: FSH, LH, estradiol, activin, and inhibin regulation of follicular maturation.
Ovarian and endometrial cycle phases; folliculogenesis, ovulation, corpus luteum formation and decline.
Estrogen/progesterone feedback and the mid-cycle LH surge.
Hormone-level timing and structural changes in ovary and endometrium.
amenorrhea: Definitions and evaluation of primary amenorrhea, secondary amenorrhea, and oligomenorrhea.
HPO-axis and anatomic causes: hypothalamic, pituitary, ovarian, endocrine, and outflow-tract disorders.
Anovulation, PCOS, hyperandrogenism, insulin resistance, and CYP17-related androgen production.
PCOS diagnostic criteria, treatment, metabolic sequelae, and unopposed-estrogen/endometrial risk.
Management by cause, including lifestyle modification, hormonal therapy, and surgery when indicated.

expected_basic_science: Hypothalamic-pituitary-ovarian axis and GnRH pulse generation.
FSH/LH regulation, estradiol/progesterone feedback, and LH surge.
Follicular maturation, ovulation, corpus luteum, ovarian and endometrial cycles.
Insulin resistance, CYP17-related ovarian androgen production, and low SHBG.
Unopposed estrogen and endometrial hyperplasia/cancer risk.
expected_clinical_science: PCOS Rotterdam criteria and differential diagnosis of amenorrhea with hyperandrogenism.
Pregnancy exclusion; interpretation of TSH, prolactin, LH/FSH, estradiol, testosterone, SHBG, 17-hydroxyprogesterone, DHEAS, metabolic tests, and pelvic ultrasound.
PCOS treatment with combined oral contraceptives, metformin, and lifestyle modification; complications including metabolic and endometrial risk.
expected_health_system_science: Access to timely specialty care and the impact of prior dismissal of menstrual concerns.
Preventive care and guideline-based cervical cancer screening.
Shared decision-making about contraceptive options and treatment goals.
expected_determinants_of_health: Family history of obesity, type 2 diabetes, infertility, hypothyroidism, dyslipidemia, and cardiac disease.
Diet, physical activity, weight concerns, and busy college schedule.
Campus housing and access barriers to specialist care.
Prior clinical dismissal and weight-related stigma.
expected_differential_diagnoses: Hypothalamic amenorrhea or functional hypothalamic causes.
Cushing syndrome and other endocrine causes.
Outflow tract disorders including Mullerian agenesis, androgen insensitivity, congenital obstruction, and Asherman syndrome.
relevant_prior_knowledge: Endocrine feedback loops and receptor signaling.
Insulin resistance, dyslipidemia, obesity, and type 2 diabetes risk.
Reproductive anatomy, puberty, and menstrual physiology.
Clinical reasoning, differential diagnosis, laboratory interpretation, and preventive care.
